name: vibraphone-a3
description: Tuned aluminium vibraphone bar, A3. Like a marimba bar the deep undercut arch tunes the transverse overtones to near-integer ratios, but the vibe is tuned one step further than the marimba's 1 : 3.9 : 9.2: the first overtone is pulled to a clean 4:1 (two octaves) and the second to 10:1, so the modes are 220 Hz * {1, 4, 10}. The audible difference from the marimba is the material, not the tuning: aluminium's internal damping is far lower than rosewood's, so the fundamental rings for several seconds where a marimba bar is gone in under two, and the upper partials — while still decaying faster than the fundamental, as they do on any struck bar — sit louder in the balance, which is the bright metallic sheen. No shape maps: strike and pickup position have no effect here, so every mode's authored gain is the amplitude a measurement of the impulse response reads back. (The vibe's real resonator tubes and motorised dampers are performance elements layered on top of this bare-bar model, not part of the bar's own modal identity.)
